pi_items.source: Cross-referenced in setup_task.sh:6-7 and export_result.sh:7. Consistent file path specification across all task scripts.
ImageJ Process Menu documentation confirms run("Subtract Background", "rolling=50") is the correct macro syntax. Verifier.py lines 60-67 checks for this command.
ImageJ Filter documentation confirms run("Median...", "radius=2") is the correct macro syntax. Verifier.py lines 71-78 checks for this command.
ImageJ Auto Threshold documentation confirms setAutoThreshold("Otsu dark") is the correct macro syntax. Verifier.py lines 82-89 checks for 'autothreshold' string in macro content (case-insensitive).
ImageJ standard post-threshold command. Verifier.py lines 93-97 checks for 'convert to mask' or 'apply' in macro content.
Task description line 5 specifies 'Rolling ball radius: 50 pixels'. ImageJ Subtract Background macro syntax uses rolling=50 parameter. Verifier.py line 60 checks for '50' in content.
Task description line 5 specifies 'Radius: 2 pixels' for Median filter. ImageJ Median macro syntax uses radius=2 parameter. Verifier.py line 71 checks for '2' in content.
Task description line 5 specifies 'Select Otsu algorithm'. ImageJ Auto Threshold uses setAutoThreshold("Otsu dark") syntax. Verifier.py line 82 checks for 'otsu' in content.

pi_items.metadata_value: File > Open Samples > Blobs (from task description)
pi_items.verified_value: Built-in ImageJ sample image available at http://imagej.nih.gov/ij/images/blobs.gif
pi_items.source: ImageJ documentation (imagej.net) and forum references confirm 'Blobs' is a standard built-in sample image (25KB) for testing and debugging macros. Accessible via File > Open Samples menu.

privileged_info_summary: The task requires recording a Fiji macro that performs three sequential image processing operations: (1) Subtract Background with rolling ball radius of 50 pixels, (2) Median filter with radius of 2 pixels, and (3) Otsu-based auto thresholding followed by Convert to Mask. The output file must be saved as 'clean_and_segment.ijm' in '/home/ga/Fiji_Data/results/macros/'. All metadata claims regarding command names, parameter values, and file paths have been verified against ImageJ/Fiji macro documentation and verified against both the task scripts and the verifier logic.
